dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6514 IH: Protecting Animals in Military Training Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Buchanan introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To prohibit the use of live animals in Department of Defense live fire trauma training.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Animals in Military Training Act.

section.header: Prohibition on use of live animals in Department of Defense live fire trauma training
section.text: Beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense shall—

paragraph.text: ensure that live animals, including dogs, cats, nonhuman primates, and marine mammals, are not used in any live fire trauma training conducted by the Department of Defense; and
in conducting such training, replace such live animals, to the extent determined necessary by the Secretary, with advanced simulators, mannequins, cadavers, or actors.
